understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to complete each level by positioning every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if a level had multiple goals, all of them needed to be occupied simultaneously for the level to report WIN, and clearing the final level would win the whole run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used directional actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) to shift the currently selected piece on a grid where each cell corresponded to 3 pixels in the reported coordinates. CLICK with a target_position in the same pixel-coordinate system switched control to another selectable piece at that exact position. Walls could block movement, but the movement.*_blocked flags were only hints (adjacent-object detection) and didn’t guarantee failure; actual collisions needed to be tested. Some adjacent objects (blocks) were pushable: attempting to move into them could push them in the same direction if space/interaction rules allowed, otherwise movement would be prevented or behave unexpectedly depending on boundary/wall behavior.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether movement is actually blocked or if walls behave unusually despite the adjacency hint.
history.reasoning: Test whether movement is actually blocked or if walls behave unusually despite the adjacency hint.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | Although all directions are flagged as blocked by walls, the hint is heuristic; try moving right to test whether this wall behaves unusually (e.g., allows pushing/warping).
history.reasoning: Although all directions are flagged as blocked by walls, the hint is heuristic; try moving right to test whether this wall behaves unusually (e.g., allows pushing/warping).

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right to see if the adjacent selectable can be pushed despite the wall hint (testing whether this corridor allows pushing).
history.reasoning: Try moving right to see if the adjacent selectable can be pushed despite the wall hint (testing whether this corridor allows pushing).

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | The current selectable is boxed in by walls, so switch control to the other selectable on the right to reach the goal area.
history.reasoning: The current selectable is boxed in by walls, so switch control to the other selectable on the right to reach the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Try stepping onto the adjacent goal tile; goals are non-collidable even if the heuristic says blocked.
history.reasoning: Try stepping onto the adjacent goal tile; goals are non-collidable even if the heuristic says blocked.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable so we can route it around the central wall toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable so we can route it around the central wall toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether moving into an apparent wall still causes a shift/teleport or other interaction from this enclosed position.
history.reasoning: Test whether moving into an apparent wall still causes a shift/teleport or other interaction from this enclosed position.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Even though all directions show blocked by walls, some levels allow slipping/teleporting when moving into walls; test moving right first to try to escape the enclosure toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Even though all directions show blocked by walls, some levels allow slipping/teleporting when moving into walls; test moving right first to try to escape the enclosure toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 9: CLICK | Current selectable is completely boxed in by walls, so switch to the other selectable near the goal to try moving it onto the + area.
history.reasoning: Current selectable is completely boxed in by walls, so switch to the other selectable near the goal to try moving it onto the + area.

history.summary: Turn 10: CLICK | Switch to the other selectable at [21,21] to test whether it can move despite the surrounding wall cells.
history.reasoning: Switch to the other selectable at [21,21] to test whether it can move despite the surrounding wall cells.

history.summary: Turn 11: CLICK | The current selectable is fully boxed in; switch to the other selectable to position it onto the goal area on the right.
history.reasoning: The current selectable is fully boxed in; switch to the other selectable to position it onto the goal area on the right.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_UP | Try moving up into the goal area; the 'blocked' hint may just indicate the goal overlay rather than a solid obstacle.
history.reasoning: Try moving up into the goal area; the 'blocked' hint may just indicate the goal overlay rather than a solid obstacle.
